frameworks.name: intuitive physics
frameworks.one_line: mental simulation of physical dynamics underlying observed agent actions

frameworks.name: intuitive psychology / theory of mind
frameworks.one_line: inference of mental states and causal role from observed action

frameworks.name: feature-based theory of moral judgment
frameworks.one_line: direct mapping from low-level visual features to moral judgments

frameworks.name: vignette-based moral psychology
frameworks.one_line: traditional approach using verbally described protagonists and intentions

core_question: Are moral judgments from dynamic visual scenes mediated by inferences over intuitive physics and intuitive psychology rather than direct visual features?
key_assumptions: observers infer mental states from observed actions
observers reason about causal contribution of agent's action to the outcome
moral judgment combines physical and mental-state inference
manipulations: dynamic visual interactions varying agent action and outcome
scene configurations distinguishing feature-based from inference-based accounts
three experiments spanning a range of dynamic interactions
key_findings: model integrating intuitive physics and intuitive psychology predicts moral judgments across diverse dynamic scenes
moral judgments are not fully explained by direct visual features
quantitative predictions match human judgments in three experiments
interpretations: visual features influence moral judgment via inferred mental states and causal role, not directly
moral judgment is mediated computation over intuitive theories of physics and mind
